concepts.title: Conjugate diameter
concepts.conceptBody:
Conjugate Diameters 

Two diameters of a hyperbola are said to be conjugate diameters if each bisects the chord parallel to the other. If ${{\text{m}}_{1}}$ and ${{\text{m}}_{2}}$ be the slopes of the conjugate diameters of a hyperbola $\frac{{{\text{x}}^{2}}}{{{\text{a}}^{2}}}-\frac{{{\text{y}}^{2}}}{\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{b}}^{2}}}=1$, then ${{\text{m}}_{1}}\text{ }\!\!~\!\!\text{ }{{\text{m}}_{2}}=\frac{{{\text{b}}^{2}}}{{{\text{a}}^{2}}}$

examples.title: The diameter of $16 x^2-9 y^2=144$, which is conjugate to $x=2 y$, is
Find the equation of that diameter which bisects the chords $7 x+y-20=0$ of the hyperbola $\frac{x^2}{3}-\frac{y^2}{7}=1$.
